config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society is strongest when individual rights are protected, because once you let “society” override them too easily, it becomes a convenient excuse for abuse by those in power. In my view, public interests matter, but they should be pursued through fair laws that limit harm without crushing personal freedom. As someone shaped by a country where collective values are important, I still believe the individual needs real protection, not just promises.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; bagi saya, hak individu harus menjadi titik awal, bukan dikorbankan demi “kepentingan masyarakat” yang sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Dari pengalaman sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi prioritas terlalu besar, kelompok kecil dan orang yang tidak populer justru paling cepat dirugikan. Tentu masyarakat butuh aturan bersama untuk keselamatan dan ketertiban, tetapi batasnya harus jelas dan proporsional, bukan memberi izin untuk menggilas kebebasan pribadi. Kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi nilainya justru lebih kuat kalau dibangun di atas perlindungan hak individu yang nyata.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of collective responsibility, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence dissent, pressure minorities, or excuse weak accountability. Society does need rules for safety, order, and fairness, but those rules are legitimate only when they protect people equally instead of treating individual rights as disposable. In practice, a society that respects personal rights is usually stronger, because people trust it more and are less afraid of being sacrificed by the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari sudut pandang Amerika, saya sangat curiga ketika “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai sebagai alasan untuk memperluas kekuasaan negara atau mayoritas, karena sejarah menunjukkan yang paling sering dirugikan justru minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer. Kepentingan sosial memang nyata, terutama untuk keselamatan dan ketertiban, tetapi saya hanya bisa menerimanya jika pembatasan itu sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu. Kalau hak individu dianggap bisa dikorbankan kapan saja, maka masyarakat tidak menjadi lebih kuat, melainkan lebih mudah menyalahgunakan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand why people emphasize harmony, order, and the common good, but I have also seen how easily those words can be used to pressure minorities or silence criticism. Public interests matter, yet they should be limited by clear rights so that the majority cannot treat some people as disposable. A society is not truly healthy if it asks individuals to sacrifice basic dignity every time someone claims it is for the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh diberi prioritas otomatis atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya cenderung sangat waspada terhadap argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” karena sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa alasan seperti itu sering dipakai untuk memperluas kuasa negara atau mayoritas dan menekan minoritas. Memang masyarakat butuh aturan untuk keselamatan dan ketertiban, tetapi aturan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu; kalau tidak, hak individu berubah menjadi sesuatu yang bisa dikorbankan kapan saja. Bagi saya, masyarakat justru lebih kuat ketika hak individu dilindungi secara nyata, bukan saat hak itu dianggap sekunder.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
